code_dossier.name: HasPermissionRequest.java
code_dossier.description: A Data Transfer Object (DTO) used for permission verification requests in the account management system. It encapsulates the user identifier and permission string required to check if a user has a specific permission.

data.detailed_description:
HasPermissionRequest is a lightweight Data Transfer Object (DTO) class located in the account-api module of the account management system. This class serves as a request payload for permission verification operations, typically used in REST API calls or service method invocations to check whether a specific user possesses a particular permission.

The class leverages Lombok's @Data annotation to automatically generate boilerplate code including getters, setters, equals(), hashCode(), and toString() methods. This approach significantly reduces code verbosity while maintaining full JavaBean compatibility.

The DTO contains two essential fields:
1. userId (Long): The unique identifier of the user whose permissions are being verified
2. permission (String): The permission code or name being checked against the user's granted permissions

This class follows the standard DTO pattern in enterprise Java applications, serving as a clean data container for cross-layer or cross-service communication in a microservices or layered architecture context.
responsibilities: Encapsulate permission check request parameters (userId and permission) into a single transferable object
Provide a standardized data structure for permission verification API requests
Enable type-safe data transfer between API consumers and the account service
Support serialization/deserialization for REST API communication
Maintain clean separation between API contract and internal domain models
